skills.name: session-startup
skills.trigger: User says 'refresh yourself', 'we're working in [project] today', or 'we shall continue'
steps: Read project README, STATUS, MEMORY, and recent git log
Summarize: current phase, what's working, what's next
Wait for user direction before acting — do not propose work
evidence: 'Refresh yourself on the mission and status' (x8 similar)
'Hello. We're working in kangaroo-court today. Please refresh yourself.'
skills.scope_reason: Generic session pattern not tied to any project

skills.name: session-wrapup
skills.trigger: User says 'commit and push', 'deploy', 'let's wrap up', or session is ending
steps: Stage and commit changes with descriptive message
Deploy to production if changes are ready
evidence: 'commit and push' (x15 similar)
'deploy to fly and vercel'

skills.name: bug-fix-iteration
skills.trigger: User pastes error logs, console output, or says 'it's still broken'
steps: Read the pasted logs carefully — identify the exact error
Propose a targeted fix (don't refactor unrelated code)
Ask user to test and report back
evidence: User pastes Docker logs, browser console output, or WebSocket errors (30+ occurrences)
'it's still not working, here's what I see now'
skills.scope_reason: References project-specific error patterns and infrastructure

skills.name: deploy-production
skills.trigger: User says 'deploy', 'push to production', or 'ship it'
steps: Deploy server to Fly.io with --local-only flag
Deploy client from client/ subdirectory to Vercel
evidence: 'deploy to fly and vercel' (x8 similar)
Documented deployment process in project README

skills.name: unity-tool-creation
skills.trigger: User describes a visual effect, camera angle, or scene element needed
steps: Create C# script for the requested feature
Add debug/test button so the feature can be tested in isolation
Integrate with existing camera or scene system
Provide instructions for testing in Unity Editor
evidence: 'I need a new camera angle for the overhead shot' (x5 similar)
'add a debug button so I can test this without needing other players'
skills.scope_reason: References Unity, C# scripts, specific game features

rules.name: no-regressions
rules.enforces: Never break existing functionality when adding new features. Test the happy path after every change.
evidence: 'you broke the camera switching again'
'the objection button stopped working after your changes'

rules.name: keep-it-simple
rules.enforces: Solve the exact problem stated. Don't add features the user didn't ask for.
evidence: 'I just wanted to fix the button, not refactor the whole component'
'too complicated — just do the simple version'

rules.name: use-refs-not-state
rules.enforces: Use refs for values accessed in callbacks/closures, not React state.
evidence: Repeated WebRTC connection issues traced to stale state in closures
'the peer connection keeps using the old value'

rules.name: secure-credentials
rules.enforces: Never paste secrets in responses or store them insecurely. Warn if credentials appear in committable files.
evidence: User shared API keys that needed secure storage
'don't echo the password back'

rules.name: step-by-step-instructions
rules.enforces: When manual action is required, provide numbered lists with exact commands. Don't explain theory.
evidence: 'just tell me what to type'
'too much explanation, just give me the steps'

rules.name: no-log-flooding
rules.enforces: Don't make changes that cause console log flooding. Add reconnection limits and debouncing.
evidence: WebSocket reconnection loops filling the console
'the logs are going crazy again'
rules.scope_reason: Related to WebSocket/real-time connection patterns in this project

rules.name: documented-deployment
rules.enforces: Follow the project's documented deployment process exactly.
evidence: Deployment failures when steps were skipped or reordered
'always cd to project root first'

rules.name: debug-buttons
rules.enforces: Provide debug/test buttons for visual features so they can be tested in isolation.
evidence: 'add a debug button so I can test this without needing other players'
'I can't test this unless I have 4 people connected'
rules.scope_reason: Specific to multiplayer game development workflow

communication_style.brevity: Terse and direct. Typical messages: 1-3 sentences, minimal punctuation, frequent lowercase.
communication_style.feedback_pattern: Reports results factually. Pastes logs when things break. Says 'it works' when things work.
communication_style.correction_style: Patient but persistent. Will paste logs repeatedly. Pushes back when AI goes in circles.
communication_style.proactivity_expectation: High. Expects the agent to execute rather than explain options.

session_patterns.startup: Opens with 'Hello. We're working in [project] today. Please refresh yourself.' or 'we shall continue'. Expects a concise project status summary before direction.
session_patterns.wrapup: Ends with 'commit and push' followed by deployment. Expects agent to handle git and deploy commands.
evidence: 'Refresh yourself on the mission and status' (x8)
'commit and push' (x15)

improvement_areas.problem: Agent sometimes modifies unrelated code when fixing bugs, causing regressions
evidence: Camera switching broke after unrelated UI changes
WebSocket handler changed during a CSS fix
improvement_areas.proposed_rule: Before modifying any file, check if it's directly related to the reported issue. If not, don't touch it.

improvement_areas.problem: Agent provides lengthy explanations when user just wants action
evidence: 'just do it' (x5)
'too much explanation'
improvement_areas.proposed_rule: Default to executing the task. Only explain if the user asks 'why' or if there's a risk that needs acknowledgment.

project_context.description: Browser-based virtual courtroom game with WebRTC multiplayer, Unity-rendered scenes, and AI-processed video. A 'Judge' player controls a broadcast-quality camera compositor streamed via Discord.
